title: Prophesy an Army into Existence
summary: “The hand of the LORD was upon me, and carried me out in the spirit of the LORD, and set me down in the midst of the valley which was full of bones, And caused me to pass by them round about: and, behold, there were very many in the open valley; and, lo, they were very dry.” Ezek 37:1-2  The first thing we ought to notice about Ezekiel’s vision of the dry bones is the fact that these bones were not buried, thankfully. They were dead and dry, but not buried. “And there is hope in thine end, saith the LORD, that thy children shall come again to their own border” (Jer 31:17). The believer does not go to the funeral of a soul – we go in the hope of resurrecting the soul in Jesus’ name! In his entire ministry, the Lord Jesus never attended funerals so the “souls could rest in peace”; he came to the tomb of Lazarus to raise him back from the dead. Every death that Jesus encountered, the soul was ordered back to the land of the living. No wonder he declared, “Jesus said unto her, I am the resurrection, and the life: he that believeth in me, though he were dead, yet shall he live” (John 11:25). We serve the God who goes to funerals and graveyards to raise the dead – let us be armed with this faith and understanding and go to meet the dead souls around us.
